item.title: Publishers attract readers with creativity, aesthetic design
item.description: Publishers, participating in the Amar Ekushey Book Fair 2026, are not only competing in book sales but also in exploring creativity. Through artistic design, architectural flair, and thematic presentation, they are striving to captivate readers. "Our highest-selling books are by a publicity-shy no

item.title: Arrest, punish Hafizur's killers, Jamaat urges govt
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami has urged the government to arrest and give exemplary punishment to the killers of Hafizur Rahman, a local leader of the party in Jibonnagar upazila under Chuadanga. Jamaat Secretary General Mia Golam Parwar, in a statement issued on Sunday, said a group of BNP supporter

item.title: Water-level fall disrupts power generation, communication
item.description: Lack of rain since December in the Chattogram Hill Tracts has decreased the water level of Kaptai Lake, severely disrupting power generation at the 230 MW Karnafuli Hydropower Station, communication and transportation of essential agricultural products. Only one among five units of the power stati

item.title: BGMEA warns factories after Indian buyer fails to clear export bill
item.description: The Bangladesh Garment Manufacturers and Exporters Association (BGMEA) has asked its member factories to refrain from doing business with four Indian companies as one of the buyers allegedly failed to pay for goods already shipped. Bangladesh travel guide The alert came after Styleverse Lifestyle

item.title: Oil surges, stocks slide as conflict grips Middle East
item.description: Oil prices surged on Monday and shares slid as military conflict in the Middle East looked set to last weeks, threatening to upend a global economic recovery and perhaps reignite inflation. Brent jumped 6.4 per cent to $77.57 a barrel, though it had briefly topped $82.00 at one stage, while US cru

item.title: BB governor appointment sparks civil society concern: Citizens’ Coalition
item.description: A citizens' platform has expressed concern over the recent appointment of a new governor of Bangladesh Bank, questioning both the selection process and the background of the appointee. In a statement sent on Monday, signed by Fahim Mashrur, co-convener of the Citizens' Coalition, the platform said

item.title: Smart, secure, inclusive
item.description: As Bangladesh enters the spiritually significant month of Ramadan, followed by the festive celebration of Eid, financial activity across the country accelerates. From remittances and retail consumption to charitable giving and digital payments, the season represents one of the largest annual econom

item.title: Light up, stock up
item.description: Ramadan arrives each year not only as a sacred month of reflection and devotion but also as a season of preparation for a renewal. Across Bangladesh, families begin planning weeks in advance how to go about making the best use of the practice of purity and fervour. They wish to organise their homes

item.title: Alauddin Al Azad appointed Religious Affairs secretary
item.description: The government has appointed retired Joint Secretary Munshi Alauddin Al Azad as Secretary of the Ministry of Religious Affairs on a contractual basis for one year. The Public Administration Ministry issued a notification in this regard on Sunday. According to the notification, Alauddin Al Azad ha

item.title: When the bonus arrives
item.description: Every year, as the moon of Ramadan wanes, a singular question occupies the minds of Bangladesh's salaried workforce: where does the Eid bonus go? Across sectors from banking and civil service to garments, telecoms, and NGOs, millions of jobholders receive their festival bonus and must decide, often

item.title: Making payments easier with credit cards
item.description: In today's digital era, cash transactions are gradually being replaced by smarter and more secure payment methods. Among them, credit cards have gained significant popularity due to their convenience, security, and added reimbursements. At Midland Bank PLC., we are witnessing a growing demand for o

item.title: Straightforward savings
item.description: City Bank's Ramadan 2026 campaign is packed but clearly structured, focusing on cashback, dining and reward points. Cashback on prominent lifestyle brands includes (a) 25 per cent cashback (maximum Tk 1,500 per transaction) for American Express Platinum Reserve cards; and (b) 10 per cent cashback (
